channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Why Claude Managed Agents Fail in Production - And How to Fix It
item.description: Straight Answer Claude Managed Agents execute multi-step tasks - tool calls, data retrieval, synthesis - but without external controls, they fail silently. No checkpoint, no validation, no...

item.title: Arquitetura Monolítica no desenvolvimento moderno
item.description: Este artigo estuda o uso da arquitetura monolítica no contexto do desenvolvimento de software moderno. Frequentemente comparada com microserviços, a arquitetura monolítica apresenta aspectos...

item.title: Self-Healing Dev Bounty Hunter with OpenClaw in Action
item.description: This is a submission for the OpenClaw Challenge. What I Built The traditional developer career path is making way for the "Digital Hermit"—engineers who reject the 9-to-5 in favor of...

item.title: Reading list (29th March to April 20th)
item.description: These are the current online posts that I enjoyed reading and made me think. AI Great video that provides an essential overview of how to build with AI (in this instance using Spring AI). Has a...

item.title: Unpacking Codeburn: Your Dashboard for AI Coding Cost Insights
item.description: As artificial intelligence continues to reshape the software development industry, understanding the costs associated with these powerful tools is essential for optimizing resource allocation....

item.title: Stop Manually Updating Version Numbers in PowerShell Projects — PSScriptBuilder Does It For You
item.description: If you maintain a PowerShell project, you've probably done this before: Bump the version in MyScript.ps1 or MyModule.psd1 Update the version comment in your script header Add the date to...

item.title: Web3 Terminology Mapped to What You Already Know
item.description: Web3 can seem intimidating at first. And a lot of that is to do with terminology. But once you make the connection between what you already know from web and mobile dev, for example, everything...

item.title: Part 2 : I got tired of wiring the same caching stack every project, so I built LayerCache
item.description: LayerCache in Production: 5 Patterns That Actually Save You Part 2 of the LayerCache series. If you missed Part 1, start here — it covers the core problem and the basic setup. The first post got...

item.title: Copy Files in PowerShell: Copy-Item with Safety
item.description: Copy Files in PowerShell: Copy-Item with Safety Duplicating files is one of the most common tasks. Learn to copy safely. How It Works Copy-Item creates a duplicate of a file or folder. The...

item.title: Localhost vs Deployment — The Pain Is Real
item.description: We’ve all been there. It works on my machine™ — until it doesn’t. On localhost, everything is perfect. APIs respond instantly. Environment variables are exactly where they should be. CORS? Never...

item.title: Fortnite developers can make AI characters now — just don’t try to date them
item.description: Following last year's AI-powered Darth Vader in Fortnite that swore in a re-creation of James Earl Jones' voice, Epic Games is now letting Fortnite creators experiment with a new "conversations"...

item.title: NSA spies are reportedly using Anthropic’s Mythos, despite Pentagon feud
item.description: NSA is said to be using Anthropic's restricted Mythos AI model.

item.title: Birdfy’s new 4K feeder will teach you about the birds it identifies
item.description: Birdfy has announced a new version of its camera-equipped metal feeder with an upgraded camera to make you feel even closer to nature while staring at your phone from the sofa. The Birdfy Feeder...

item.title: Humanoid ‘Lightning’ robot smashes the half-marathon record
item.description: An autonomous scarlet robot named "Lightning" finished a 13-mile race in Beijing on Sunday in just 50 minutes and 26 seconds, less than half the time of last year's fastest robot, which took two...

item.title: CEO and CFO suddenly depart AI nuclear power upstart Fermi
item.description: The startup, co-founded by former U.S. Energy Secretary Rick Perry, has faced headwinds with its AI campus in Texas.

item.title: Blue Origin’s New Glenn rocket grounded after delivering satellite to wrong orbit
item.description: The Federal Aviation Administration grounded Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket after it shuttled its payload to the wrong orbit during its launch on Sunday, according to a report from the Orlando...

item.title: Tesla launches robotaxis in Dallas and Houston, and oops, it’s already unavailable
item.description: Tesla claims to have launched a robotaxi service in Dallas and Houston over the weekend, but so far, online crowdsourcing tools indicate that very few cars are actually available. On Saturday,...

item.title: Canva’s CEO on its big pivot to AI enterprise software
item.description: Today, I’m talking with Melanie Perkins, founder and CEO of Canva, a popular online design tool. I always enjoy talking with Melanie. She was last on the show a couple of years ago, just as the AI...

item.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: Vercel Hack, Push Fraud, QEMU Abused, New Android RATs Emerge & More
item.description: Monday’s recap shows the same pattern in different places. A third-party tool becomes a way in, then leads to internal access. A trusted download path is briefly swapped to deliver malware....

item.title: The Elden Ring movie hits theaters in March 2028
item.description: It's going to be a little while before The Lands Between is realized in movie form. Bandai Namco just confirmed that the live-action Elden Ring movie, which was announced last year, is expected to...

item.title: Prego Has a Dinner-Conversation-Recording Device, Capisce?
item.description: The pasta-sauce company has partnered with the nonprofit StoryCorps on a device designed to record family conversations around the table and save them for all time.

item.title: Vampire Survivors’ new spinoff switches genres but keeps the good vibes
item.description: When Vampire Survivors first exploded onto the scene, it was pretty much all I could think about. The formula of jumping into runs, taking on thousands of enemies, and becoming absurdly...

item.title: This pasta sauce wants to record your family
item.description: As if there weren't already enough devices listening in on everything being said in your home, Prego, the pasta and pizza sauce brand, is releasing a device designed to record everything said...

item.title: Huawei beats Samsung and Apple to market with the first wide foldable
item.description: Huawei has launched its passport-style foldable in China, ahead of similar devices said to be coming from Apple and Samsung. That makes the Pura X Max the first wide foldable phone that's actually...

item.title: The Download: murderous ‘mirror’ bacteria, and Chinese workers fighting AI doubles
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. No one’s sure if synthetic mirror life will kill us all In...

item.title: Why Most AI Deployments Stall After the Demo
item.description: The fastest way to fall in love with an AI tool is to watch the demo. Everything moves quickly. Prompts land cleanly. The system produces impressive outputs in seconds. It feels like the beginning...

item.title: Is “Satoshi Nakamoto” Really Adam Back?
item.description: The New York Times has a long article where the author lays out an impressive array of circumstantial evidence that the inventor of Bitcoin is the cypherpunk Adam Back. I don’t know. The article...

item.title: Tech CEOs Think AI Will Let Them Be Everywhere at Once
item.description: Mark Zuckerberg and Jack Dorsey have different visions for how to use AI for management purposes, but both imagine a system of heightened control.

item.title: Anthropic MCP Design Vulnerability Enables RCE, Threatening AI Supply Chain
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a critical "by design" weakness in the Model Context Protocol's (MCP) architecture that could pave the way for remote code execution and have a cascading...

item.title: Colossal Biosciences said it cloned red wolves. Is it for real?
item.description: If you want to capture something wolflike, it’s best to embark before dawn. So on a morning this January, with the eastern horizon still pink-hued, I drove with two young scientists into a blanket...
